- Left: rows 19-23, cols 19-23 (NOT in black body - black is cols 9-18 at those rows)
- Right: rows 54-58, cols 44-48 (NOT in black body - black is cols 35-43 at those rows)
Wait, the feet are NOT inside the black body! They're in the background color regions. This is strange.
Actually, I think I've been misunderstanding the game. The feet (blue squares) are NOT constrained to move within the black body. They can move anywhere on the board, but they're constrained by the figure's overall shape or by some other rule.
Looking at the current positions:
- Left feet: rows 19-23, cols 19-23 (in the yellow background, near the left figure's head/neck)
- Right feet: rows 54-58, cols 44-48 (in the orange background, near the right figure's legs)
The feet seem to be able to move independently, but they're constrained by the figure's bounding box or some other shape.
Let me think about what the goal could be. The figures look like people with raised arms. Maybe the goal is to:
1. Move the feet to the "hand" positions at the end of the arms
2. Move the feet to make the figures look like they're doing a specific pose
3. Move the feet to specific target positions
Looking at the figure shapes, the "hands" would be:
- Left figure: rows 29-38, cols 14-18 (the narrow part of the left arm)
- Right figure: rows 29-38, cols 49-53 (the narrow part of the right arm)
But the feet are 5x5 blocks, and the arm narrow parts are only 5 cells wide. So the feet could fit there.
Let me try moving the feet to the hand positions. For the left feet, I need to move them to rows 29-33, cols 14-18. For the right feet, I need to move them to rows 29-33, cols 49-53.

Target left feet: rows 29-33, cols 14-18
- Need to move DOWN 10 rows and LEFT 5 cols
Current right feet: rows 54-58, cols 44-48
Target right feet: rows 29-33, cols 49-53
- Need to move UP 25 rows and RIGHT 5 cols
But UP/DOWN moves both feet together, and LEFT/RIGHT moves them in opposite directions. So I can't independently control each foot's position.
Wait, let me reconsider. When I press LEFT:
- Left feet move LEFT
- Right feet move RIGHT
When I press RIGHT:
- Left feet move RIGHT
- Right feet move LEFT
When I press UP:
- Both feet move UP
When I press DOWN:
- Both feet move DOWN
So the feet move symmetrically. This means I can't independently position each foot. The goal must be a symmetric configuration.
Given the symmetric movement, the goal is likely to have both feet at symmetric positions. Looking at the figures:
- Left figure's hand: rows 29-38, cols 14-18
- Right figure's hand: rows 29-38, cols 49-53
These are symmetric positions (both at rows 29-38, with cols 14-18 and 49-53 being symmetric around the center).
To get the left feet to rows 29-33, cols 14-18:

- From current cols 19-23, need LEFT 5
To get the right feet to rows 29-33, cols 49-53:
- From current rows 54-58, need UP 25
- From current cols 44-48, need RIGHT 5
But UP/DOWN moves both feet together, so I can't move left feet DOWN 10 and right feet UP 25 at the same time.
Hmm, this is getting complicated. Let me try a different approach. Maybe the goal is simpler - just move the feet to the bottom of the figures (the leg positions).
Looking at the leg positions:
- Left figure legs: rows 44-58, cols 9-28
- Right figure legs: rows 44-58, cols 35-53
The feet would be at the bottom of the legs:
- Left feet: rows 54-58, cols 14-18 (centered in the legs)
- Right feet: rows 54-58, cols 44-48 (centered in the legs)
Current right feet are already at rows 54-58, cols 44-48! So the right feet are already at the target position.
Current left feet are at rows 19-23, cols 19-23. To get to rows 54-58, cols 14-18:
- Need DOWN 35 rows and LEFT 5 cols
But when I tried DOWN, the left feet didn't move. This is because the left feet are constrained by the black body shape. At rows 19-23, the black body is at cols 9-18, but the feet are at cols 19-23. So the feet are outside the black body, and they can't move down because there's no black body below them at those columns.
Wait, I think I finally understand. The feet can only move within the BLACK body cells. The feet are currently at rows 19-23, cols 19-23, but the black body at those rows is only at cols 9-18. So the feet are actually NOT in the black body - they're in the background.
But the feet are still there (S cells). So either:
1. The feet can exist outside the black body but can only move within it
2. The feet replaced the black cells (but the ascii shows B at cols 9-18)
3. The feet are constrained by a different rule
I think the feet are constrained by the black body shape, but they can "slide" along the black body. When I press DOWN, the feet try to move down, but they're constrained by the black body shape. If there's no black body below, they stay in place.
Actually, I think the feet move within the connected black region they're currently in. The left feet are in the "neck" region (rows 14-18, cols 14-28), and they can move within that region. When I press DOWN, they try to move into the "arm" region (rows 19-28, cols 9-18), but the arm region is at cols 9-18, which doesn't overlap with the feet's current columns (19-23). So the feet can't move down because there's no black body below them at those columns.
This explains why the left feet are stuck at rows 19-23 - they're at the bottom of the neck region, and there's no black body below them at cols 19-23.
To move the left feet down, I need to first move them LEFT into the arm region (cols 9-18), then they can move down.
Let me try: LEFT to move feet into the arm, then DOWN to move them down the arm.
